title: G1: finstack balance_sheet / key_ratios omit banking fields (CASA/NIM/tier-1/GNPA/deposits/loans) — cross-market
description:
Confirmed 2026-04-22 in M0.3 smoke test on HDFCBANK.NS (India) and JPM (US). finstack-mcp 0.10.0:

balance_sheet(HDFCBANK | JPM):
  - NO total_deposits, casa_ratio, tier_1_ratio / cet1_ratio / car, gnpa, nnpa, nim, loans, allowance_for_loan_losses.
  - Returns generic US-GAAP-style bank aggregates only (cash_cash_equivalents_and_federal_funds_sold, trading_securities, held_to_maturity_securities, investments_and_advances).

key_ratios(HDFCBANK | JPM):
  - Same generic shape as non-bank tickers (pe, pb, p/s, roe, roa, margins).
  - NO NIM / CASA / GNPA / tier-1 / CET-1 / CAR substitutes.
  - gross_margin and ebitda_margin = 0 (meaningless for a bank).
  - debt_to_equity / current_ratio / quick_ratio / free_cash_flow silently null for banks.

income_statement is bank-flavoured and OK (interest_income / interest_expense / net_interest_income / salaries / occupancy are present) — only balance_sheet and key_ratios are gap surfaces.

Evidence: research/mcp-smoke-test-india.md §2 C08/C11, research/mcp-smoke-test-us.md §2 C08/C11. Canonical matrix row: research/mcp-gaps.md G1.

Downstream impact: M3 banking_nbfc scheme fixture cannot be sourced from finstack. SPEC §15.3 allows hand-curated fixtures (e.g., tests/scoring-engine/hdfcbank.json seeded from Screener.in). Phase 2 bank analytics also downgraded until M3 decides fixture source.

title: Holdings-only mode: redesign SPEC and implementation.md
description:
The user has chosen holdings-only maintenance in Wealthfolio (no transactions, dividends, or corp-action entries). This invalidates several SPEC assumptions and collapses the core design premise of the corp-actions-monitor skill (M5). Edit SPEC.md and implementation.md before M5 starts; can run in parallel with M0.1 since the schema dump is mode-independent.

Decided 2026-04-21 in M0.1 planning conversation. Alternatives considered: mixed mode (log only SPLIT+DIVIDEND) and transactions-eventually; both rejected on maintenance-ergonomics grounds.
design:
## docs/SPEC.md edits

- §2 bullet 1: soften. Positions first; transactions/dividends/corp-actions described as optional, user-driven enrichment.
- §5 (Wealthfolio boundary): add 'Holdings-only mode' subsection listing which downstream features degrade (M5 semantics, long-window TWR) vs remain fully functional (research pipeline, scoring, personas, reports, our own ledger).
- §5.3: note that list-transactions-since, list-dividends-since, list-splits-since are allowed to return empty; callers MUST tolerate this and not error.
- §6.2 corp-actions-monitor: rewrite. With no activities baseline, all MCP-detected events default to 'info' severity. The skill shifts from reconciliation to informational digest. §7.3 activity-type mapping stays as guidance for user manual entry.
- §12 benchmarking: add caveat that Wealthfolio native TWR is transaction-derived. In holdings-only mode, long windows emit 'insufficient history' until daily_account_valuation snapshots accumulate. Graceful-degradation output shape specified.
- §13 corp-actions behavior: same rewrite as §6.2. Remove the 'unrecorded event' severity ladder; replace with informational classification.

## docs/implementation.md edits

- M5 deliverables + acceptance: redesign as informational digest. Drop the 'IEX 1:1 bonus unrecorded' load-bearing fixture. New acceptance: tool lists upcoming + past-90-day events with sources; never emits high-severity 'unrecorded' warnings unless activities table is non-empty.
- M6: add graceful-degradation acceptance for windows exceeding snapshot history.
- M8 Phase 1 step 7: tolerate empty transactions-snapshot.csv; don't fail the phase.
- M2: list-transactions-since etc. must return empty-but-valid output when activities is empty, not error.
acceptance_criteria: docs/SPEC.md §2, §5, §5.3, §6.2, §12, §13 reflect holdings-only as the supported maintenance mode. docs/implementation.md M2, M5, M6, M8 acceptance criteria updated accordingly. A grep for 'activities table' in SPEC surfaces either a conditional clause or a note that the table may be empty. M5 no longer has any acceptance test that presumes recorded activities.
notes: Execute before M5 starts. Can run in parallel with M0.1. Once this lands, config/wealthfolio.md (in M0.1) should reference the holdings-only maintenance guidance.
close_reason: Implemented. SPEC.md §5.2 holdings-only declaration added; §6.2/§13 rewritten as informational feed (no severity); §7.3 activity-type mapping deleted with §7.4-§7.7 renumbered to §7.3-§7.6; §10.2 phase-1 step 7 removed; §14.2 TWR retargeted to daily_account_valuation; §17 directory layout cleaned; §18.1/§18.4 skill contracts updated. implementation.md M2/M5/M6/M8 updated accordingly. Follow-up bead edits (M5/M6/M8 descriptions, M2→M5 edge drop) applied. Plan files deleted.

title: M0: Foundations and audit
description:
Goal: The project has pinned external dependencies, verified upstream schemas and APIs, and a trimmed agent constitution before any runtime code is written.

Dependencies: none.

See docs/implementation.md section M0 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- sqlite3 -readonly $WEALTHFOLIO_DB '.schema' succeeds and matches the committed dump.
- For each MCP, at least one tool call from a fresh Claude/Cursor session returns non-empty data for RELIANCE.NS and AAPL.
- python -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('input/india/philosophy.md').read().split('---')[1])" parses without error and contains thresholds.non_financial.roe_min.
- CLAUDE.md line count <= 80 (excluding the Beads integration block).
notes:
Risks:
- Wealthfolio schema drift between release cadence and our pin (SPEC §19.14). Mitigation: the pin is explicit; any Wealthfolio upgrade is its own ticket.
- MCP India coverage gaps surface before M5. Mitigation: log gaps into research/mcp-gaps.md and revisit in M5/M14.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: M1.10: shellcheck + final acceptance
description: Run shellcheck skills/scripts/*.sh (including _claim-common.sh), fix any violations. Run all M1 tests end-to-end (concurrent.sh, stale-reclaim.sh, completion-validation tests, validate-prerequisites tests). Verify all 4 acceptance criteria from implementation.md §M1: (1) 3-process concurrent test — 15 tickers, exactly-one-agent, no collisions. (2) complete-scoring.sh INFY.NS exits non-zero when ## Scoring missing. (3) reclaim-stale.sh 30 releases aged locks, leaves fresh. (4) shellcheck clean.
acceptance_criteria: 1. shellcheck skills/scripts/*.sh exits 0. 2. tests/claim-ctl/concurrent.sh passes. 3. tests/claim-ctl/stale-reclaim.sh passes. 4. All completion-validation + prerequisites tests pass. 5. All 4 implementation.md §M1 acceptance criteria met.
notes: Source: M1-investigation.md §6.8 (shellcheck). implementation.md §M1 acceptance criteria.

title: M1.9: skills/scripts/README.md
description: Author skills/scripts/README.md covering: (1) local-FS caveat — must run from local filesystem, NFS forbidden, (2) per-ticker time budget — single ticker must complete within stale threshold (default 30 min), (3) SIGKILL recovery — reclaim-stale <mins> is the sole recovery command, (4) command summary — one-line description per subcommand with SPEC §18.8 signature, (5) execution venues — native and via bin/run-skill, (6) .claimed/.done payload format and CLAIM_MODEL_ID env var, (7) agent-id convention — URL-safe string, no whitespace, no =.
notes: Source: M1-investigation.md §7.6 (README outline). Written after all scripts exist to ensure accuracy.

title: M1.8: Completion-validation + prerequisites tests
description: Four test scripts under tests/claim-ctl/: (1) complete-scoring-missing.sh — stock file lacks ## Scoring; complete-scoring INFY.NS exits non-zero, no .done written. (2) complete-scoring-ok.sh — stock file has ## Scoring; exits 0, .done written. (3) complete-persona-ok.sh — stock file has ## Persona Cross-Check; exits 0, .done written. (4) validate-prerequisites.sh test — seeds stock files with/without [Fund]+[Valu] tags and ## Scoring block; validates exit codes for phases 4 and 5.
notes: Source: M1-investigation.md §4 (completion-validation tests). Covers hook interface (seam 1 resolved), validate-prerequisites (seam 3 resolved).

title: M1.7: Stale-reclaim test
description: tests/claim-ctl/stale-reclaim.sh — seeds 3 ticker dirs, claims all 3 via claim-stocks 3 agent-A, backdates one .claimed mtime by 31 minutes (BSD: date -v-31M, GNU: date -d '31 min ago' — test detects platform), runs reclaim-stale 30, asserts: (1) only the aged .claimed is removed, (2) the other two remain, (3) claim-stocks 3 agent-B re-claims exactly the aged ticker.
notes: Source: M1-investigation.md §4 (stale-reclaim test). BSD/GNU date handling per §4 note.

title: M1.6: Concurrent claim test
description: tests/claim-ctl/concurrent.sh — seeds 15 ticker dirs (T01..T15) under temp/research/claims/, spawns 3 background subshells each calling claim-stocks 5 agent-{A,B,C}, waits, then asserts: (1) exactly 15 .claimed files exist, (2) union of claimed tickers has cardinality 15 (coverage), (3) no ticker has >1 claim (exclusivity via O_EXCL), (4) agent IDs inside each .claimed are one of {A, B, C}. Outcome-based assertions, not timing-based.
notes: Source: M1-investigation.md §4 (concurrent test design). Runs natively on host APFS.

title: M1.5: validate-prerequisites.sh
description: Standalone script (does NOT source _claim-common.sh). Takes a phase number argument: 'validate-prerequisites 4' checks every temp/research/stocks/{TICKER}.md for [Fund] and [Valu] tag lines; 'validate-prerequisites 5' checks for ## Scoring block. Exit non-zero on any missing prerequisite; print offending tickers to stderr. Per SPEC §18.8.
notes: See plans/M1/M1.5-validate-prerequisites.md
close_reason: validate-prerequisites.sh implemented; phase 4 checks ^[Fund] and ^[Valu] tag headers; phase 5 checks ^## Scoring header; loud failure on missing/empty stocks dir; exit codes split 1=data 2=usage; bash 3.2 portable (mapfile→while-read deviation logged in plan); shellcheck/parse/14-row smoke green; full acceptance via M1.8/M1.10

title: M1.4: Phase 5 persona claim shims
description: Write 4 thin shims with CLAIM_ROOT=temp/research/persona-claims/: claim-persona.sh, complete-persona.sh, check-persona-progress.sh, reclaim-persona-stale.sh. complete-persona.sh hooks the ## Persona Cross-Check header validation: grep -qE '^## Persona Cross-Check[[:space:]]*$' on the stock file. Each shim includes shellcheck source directive.
notes: See plans/M1/M1.4-phase5-persona-shims.md
close_reason: Phase 5 persona shims (claim-persona/complete-persona/check-persona-progress/reclaim-persona-stale) implemented. complete-persona rejects when '## Persona Cross-Check' header missing on stock file; smoke against temp tree green (header-missing -> exit 1 no .done; header-present -> exit 0 .done; reclaim-persona-stale removes aged .claimed). bash -n parse OK on all four; shellcheck deferred to M1.10 epic-level sweep. Tests M1.6/M1.7/M1.8 are downstream. Plan: plans/M1/M1.4-phase5-persona-shims.md. Dep direction fixed: M1.4 now blocks M1.8/M1.9 (was inverted).

title: M1.3: Phase 4 scoring claim shims
description: Write 4 thin shims with CLAIM_ROOT=temp/research/scoring-claims/: claim-scoring.sh, complete-scoring.sh, check-scoring-progress.sh, reclaim-scoring-stale.sh. complete-scoring.sh hooks the ## Scoring header validation: grep -qE '^## Scoring[[:space:]]*$' on the stock file. Rejects and exits non-zero if header absent (no .done written). Each shim includes shellcheck source directive.
notes: See plans/M1/M1.3-phase4-scoring-shims.md
close_reason: Phase 4 scoring shims (claim-scoring/complete-scoring/check-scoring-progress/reclaim-scoring-stale) implemented. complete-scoring rejects when '## Scoring' header missing on stock file; smoke against temp tree green (header-missing -> exit 1 no .done; header-present -> exit 0 .done; reclaim-scoring-stale removes aged .claimed). shellcheck noise matches the M1.2 Phase 2 baseline (SC1091/SC2034 from sourced library); final sweep gate is M1.10. Tests M1.6/M1.7/M1.8 are downstream.

title: M1.2: Phase 2 claim shims
description: Write 4 thin shims that set CLAIM_ROOT=temp/research/claims/ and delegate to _claim-common.sh: claim-stocks.sh, complete-stock.sh, check-progress.sh, reclaim-stale.sh. No completion validation hook for Phase 2 (just write .done). Each shim includes '# shellcheck source=./_claim-common.sh' directive. CLI signatures per SPEC §18.8.
notes: See plans/M1/M1.2-phase2-shims.md
close_reason: Phase 2 shims (claim-stocks/complete-stock/check-progress/reclaim-stale) implemented; bash -n + 7-step smoke against CLAIM_CTL_ROOT temp tree all green (lex-order claim, progress summary, .done writeback, stale reclaim). shellcheck not installed on host; deferred to M1.10 in Docker per the M1.1 close-note pattern. Beads dep direction flipped: M1.6/M1.7/M1.9 now correctly depend on M1.2.

title: M1.1: _claim-common.sh shared library
description: Implement the parameterized claim/release/reclaim/progress primitives in skills/scripts/_claim-common.sh using set -o noclobber + O_CREAT|O_EXCL. Covers: .claimed/.done sentinel lifecycle (§6.7 payload format: agent_id, claimed_at_epoch, model_id from CLAIM_MODEL_ID env var), mtime-based stale detection (find -mmin / stat), completion-validation hook interface for Phase 4/5 shims to plug into. Parameterized on CLAIM_ROOT and an optional validation callback. All functions use set -euo pipefail, quoted variables, [[ ]] over [ ]. Behavior details per M1-investigation.md §6 (ticker-pool scan, lex sort, batch-size edge cases, .done availability rule, complete-* prerequisite check, idempotency, check-progress output shape).
notes: See plans/M1/M1.1-claim-common-lib.md
close_reason: _claim-common.sh shared library implemented; 4 public functions (_claim_batch/_claim_complete/_claim_check_progress/_claim_reclaim_stale) verified end-to-end with smoke tests. shellcheck verification deferred to M1.10 (Docker not currently running on host). Beads dep direction flipped: M1.2/M1.3/M1.4 now correctly depend on M1.1.

title: G7: nsekit corporate_actions(period='3M') under-reports events
description:
Confirmed 2026-04-22 in M0.3 India smoke test. nsekit-mcp 0.0.22:

corporate_actions(RELIANCE, period="3M") -> empty (genuinely: no events in window; 1Y probe returned 1 event).
corporate_actions(HDFCBANK, period="3M") -> empty.
  BUT: nsekit equity_price_history(HDFCBANK, "1Y") shows a large price step on 19-Mar-2026 (prev_close 843 -> open 770, close 798.2) consistent with an ex-date event within the 3M window that should have been reported.
corporate_actions(INFY, period="3M") -> empty.

Evidence: research/mcp-smoke-test-india.md §2 C22/C23/C24 + price evidence C20. Canonical matrix row: research/mcp-gaps.md G7.

Hypothesis: either the "3M" enum is interpreted as a shorter window than 90 days upstream, or the event feed genuinely does not include HDFCBANK's recent event. M5 should:
  1. Re-probe with explicit start_date / end_date (today -90d .. today +14d) rather than the period enum.
  2. If still empty, fall back to Yahoo `actions` per SPEC §6.2 / ai-portfolio-manager-ap0 (holdings-only semantics already allow Yahoo-fallback with source attribution).

Downstream impact: M5 corp-actions-monitor is informational only (ap0 decision) — this is not a correctness risk, only a source-coverage risk. Acceptable for Phase 1 with Yahoo fallback. Revisit if NseKit ships a fix before M5 starts.

title: G9: finstack sec_filing multi-year lookback returns fewer filings than requested for JPM
description:
Confirmed 2026-04-22 in M0.3 US smoke test. finstack-mcp 0.10.0:

sec_filing(JPM, filing_type="10-K", count=5):
  - Returns count=1. Only latest 10-K (filed 2026-02-13 for FY2025).
  - Prior FY2024/2023/2022/2021 10-Ks exist on EDGAR but are not returned.

sec_filing(JPM, filing_type="10-Q", count=5):
  - Returns count=3. Only 2025 quarters (Q1/Q2/Q3). Older 10-Qs absent.

Contrast:
  - sec_filing(AAPL, 10-K, count=5) -> 5 filings returned (2021-10-29 through 2025-10-31).
  - sec_filing(MSFT, 10-K, count=5) -> 5 filings returned.
  - sec_filing(AAPL, 10-Q, count=5) -> 5 filings.
  - sec_filing(MSFT, 10-Q, count=5) -> 5 filings.

Tool signature (symbol, filing_type, count) matches SPEC §18.2 — only the ticker-level lookback depth is off, specifically for JPM. May affect other large-bank filers (BAC, WFC, C) — untested.

Evidence: research/mcp-smoke-test-us.md §2 C14/C17. Canonical matrix row: research/mcp-gaps.md G9.

Downstream impact (M9): Phase 2 US Tier-1 most-recent-filing flow is NOT blocked (latest 10-K/10-Q is returned). Multi-year 10-K diffing / year-over-year filing analytics requires a fallback: direct EDGAR `browse-edgar?CIK=<cik>&type=10-K` call to enumerate prior filings when finstack returns count < requested.

title: G4: finstack routes Indian ADR-listed tickers (INFY etc.) to US ADR in USD, not NSE INR
description:
Confirmed 2026-04-22 in M0.3 India smoke test. finstack-mcp 0.10.0:

For INFY (NSE-listed, also NYSE ADR-listed):
- company_profile(INFY) -> exchange="NYQ", currency="USD", market_cap in USD (~55.56B), not NSE/INR.
- income_statement(INFY) -> currency="USD".
- balance_sheet(INFY) -> currency="USD".
- key_ratios(INFY) -> currency="USD".

RELIANCE and HDFCBANK return exchange="NSI" and currency="INR" correctly (they are not ADR-listed).

nsekit equity_price_history(INFY, "1Y") does return NSE INR price data — viable fallback path for INFY price/quote data.

Evidence: research/mcp-smoke-test-india.md §2 C03/C06/C09/C12/C21. Canonical matrix row: research/mcp-gaps.md G4.

Downstream impact (M9 fundamentals-fetch aggregator): when an Indian ticker is ADR-listed, finstack returns US ADR financials in USD which do not match the SPEC §18.2 India shape (INR, Indian AS). Aggregator must either:
  (a) detect ADR routing and fall back to Tier-B / Yahoo / web for fundamentals (SPEC §15), or
  (b) convert USD figures to INR at as_of FX and flag the data provenance in sources[] / missing_fields.

Recommended: (a). ADR detection heuristic: finstack company_profile(symbol).exchange == "NYQ" AND country == "India" => ADR routing. Known ADR-listed Indian names to consider: INFY, WIT (Wipro), HDB (HDFC Bank NYSE ADR), IBN (ICICI Bank ADR), RDY (Dr. Reddy's), TTM (Tata Motors).

title: M0.11: Rewrite AGENTS.md as portfolio-manager constitution
description: Replace the current dev-guardrails content in AGENTS.md with the portfolio-manager runtime agent constitution. Hoist the 5 critical SPEC §19 invariants to the top: (1) agent never writes Wealthfolio DB, (2) Phase 1 snapshot is frozen, (3) deterministic math vs LLM narrative, (4) immediate-action cap = 2, (5) no fabrication. Reference the remaining SPEC §19 invariants by number. Keep under 80 lines. CLAUDE.md stays untouched per user decision (dev guardrails remain there; merge with AGENTS.md deferred). This deviates from M0 spec acceptance criterion which named CLAUDE.md — the swap is intentional and user-approved.
close_reason: AGENTS.md rewritten as pure portfolio-manager runtime constitution: 5 critical invariants at top, all 21 SPEC §19 invariants referenced, phase commands table, 48 lines, no dev guardrails, no beads block

Cross-reference SPEC §10 phase definitions.

Compose mounts the repo and the Wealthfolio SQLite DB as read-only; Wealthfolio and Claude stay on the host. Verify docker compose build succeeds. Depends on M0.1 (DB path for mount).

Add a '-- version: wealthfolio v3.2.1' header. Depends on M0.1.
close_reason: skills/sql/wealthfolio-queries.sql v1 skeleton authored per plans/M0/M0.7-sql-skeleton.md. Six named queries aligned with SPEC §5.3 + §6.8: export-snapshot, list-holdings, get-cash-balance, get-net-worth, get-avg-cost, get-portfolio-twr. Holdings-only — no activities reads. Version-pinned to Wealthfolio v3.2.1 via header. All 10 static verification checks pass (6 named queries, 0 DDL/DML, 0 activities refs, 0 positional placeholders, only pinned tables referenced, 21 TODO(M2) flags deferring Position JSON field names, multi-ccy cash, TWR chaining, and taxonomy-based asset_type inference to M2 wrapper execution against seeded fixture.

title: M0.4: Author THIRD_PARTY.md
description: Assemble THIRD_PARTY.md at repo root with exact-version pins for Wealthfolio release, virattt/ai-hedge-fund SHA, finstack-mcp, NseKit-MCP, nse-bse-mcp. Each entry must include license text and a one-line rationale for the pin. Depends on M0.1, M0.2, M0.3.
notes: Delivered THIRD_PARTY.md at repo root (191 lines). All 15 automated verification checks pass; cross-links resolve. Deviation from plan step 2: upstream repos for finstack-mcp and nse-bse-mcp ship no git tags matching the pinned PyPI/npm version (finstack has only v0.3.2; nse-bse has none), so LICENSE text was captured from the pinned release artifacts (finstack sdist, nse-bse npm tarball) instead of via curl against a tag URL. Strictly stronger evidence — the LICENSE file inside the pinned package IS the release's own license artifact. Attribution lines in §3.1 and §3.3 record the artifact URL and fetch date. See plans/M0/M0.4-third-party.md for full plan.

title: M0.3: MCP research, config, and smoke test
description: Research actual repos/packages for finstack-mcp, NseKit-MCP, nse-bse-mcp (confirm installability or identify alternatives). Author config/mcp-servers.md with setup docs, ship a .claude/settings.json template with entries, run a smoke test calling at least one tool on each MCP for RELIANCE.NS and AAPL from a fresh session, log outputs to research/mcp-smoke-test.md, log coverage gaps to research/mcp-gaps.md. Per user: config-only, no vendoring; MCPs installed however the user prefers.
notes:
Agent steps 1-4 of plans/M0/M0.3-mcp-servers.md complete (2026-04-22):

1. Versions re-queried; no drift vs the 2026-04-18 investigation — pinned:
   - finstack-mcp == 0.10.0 (MIT)
   - nsekit-mcp   == 0.0.22 (no license, risk recorded)
   - nse-bse-mcp  ==  0.1.5 (MIT)
2. Registration templates shipped:
   - .mcp.json           (Claude Code, project-scoped)
   - .cursor/mcp.json    (Cursor, project-scoped)
   Both carry finstack + nsekit + nse-bse under {"mcpServers": ...}.
   Deviation from bd wording: bd description mentioned .claude/settings.json; current Claude Code MCP registration is .mcp.json. .claude/settings.json is left untouched (holds bd hooks already).
3. Minor template choice: finstack uses "uvx finstack-mcp@0.10.0" (not "python -m finstack.server" from the plan draft) so the version pins inline and no pre-install is required. Matches nsekit's uvx shape. Alt pre-install route documented in config/mcp-servers.md §2.
4. config/mcp-servers.md follows the 7-section outline (89 lines, under the ~140 target). License risk language is verbatim-ready for M0.4 to copy into THIRD_PARTY.md §5.
5. temp/ added to .gitignore for the scratch paste files.
6. Smoke-test prompt scripts produced:
   - plans/M0/M0.3-smoke-script-india.md  (24 cells: 8 tools x RELIANCE.NS / HDFCBANK.NS / INFY.NS)
   - plans/M0/M0.3-smoke-script-us.md     (18 cells: 6 tools x AAPL / JPM / MSFT)

HANDOFF — user-executed next (plan steps 5-6):
  a. India session: start `npx -y nse-bse-mcp@0.1.5`, open a fresh Cursor/Claude window, paste plans/M0/M0.3-smoke-script-india.md prompts, capture to temp/mcp-smoke-raw-india.md.
  b. US session: fresh window, paste plans/M0/M0.3-smoke-script-us.md prompts, capture to temp/mcp-smoke-raw-us.md.

Returning to agent (plan step 7): parse both scratch files into research/mcp-smoke-test-india.md, research/mcp-smoke-test-us.md, and research/mcp-gaps.md (or the empty-but-valid variant). If any banking cell (HDFCBANK.NS or JPM) fails get_balance_sheet / get_ratios with non-banking shape, file a bd follow-up before closing.

Pre-smoke verification-table checks (run now) all pass:

- jq keys on .mcp.json and .cursor/mcp.json                  ["finstack","nse-bse","nsekit"] both
- version-pin grep                                           4 matches (>= 2)
- NseKit license risk recorded                               2 hits in config/mcp-servers.md

This ticket stays OPEN pending smoke execution.

=== 2026-04-22 close — M0.3 complete ===

Plan steps 7-9 (agent post-smoke) executed:

description: Install Wealthfolio locally on macOS, document the install path, locate the SQLite DB, dump .schema to research/wealthfolio-schema-<release>.txt, and author config/wealthfolio.md covering install steps + DB path + Docker volume mount instructions. Required by SPEC §5 and M0 deliverables. Per user: install step is guided; document the official install path during the task.
notes: See plans/M0/M0.1-wealthfolio.md for full plan. Version pin: v3.2.1 (build 20260301.1). Docker mount: Option A (read-only bind mount of ~/Library/Application Support/com.teymz.wealthfolio → /wealthfolio). Holdings-only maintenance mode chosen — config/wealthfolio.md §2 wording may need reconciliation after ai-portfolio-manager-ap0 lands.
close_reason: Schema dumped to research/wealthfolio-schema-v3.2.1.txt (475 lines, 34 CREATE TABLE, 0 data rows). config/wealthfolio.md authored with all 8 sections. All 7 acceptance checks from plan verification table pass. Unblocks M0.4, M0.7, M0.9.

title: M0.2: Pin virattt/ai-hedge-fund commit SHA
description: Pick the latest green-main commit SHA from virattt/ai-hedge-fund, capture MIT license text, and record the pin rationale. Feeds THIRD_PARTY.md (M0.4) and the M3 scoring-engine port.
notes: See plans/M0/M0.2-ai-hedge-fund-pin.md · Manifest at research/ai-hedge-fund-pin-0f6ac48.md · Pinned 0f6ac487986f7eb80749ed42bd26fb8330c450db (HEAD identical to research SHA; ahead_by=0, behind_by=0 on 2026-04-21) · All 10 port-target files HTTP 200 · MIT captured with reconstructed copyright line + LICENSE-missing disclosure
close_reason: Closed

title: M15: US market activation
description:
Goal: /phase1..7 market=us runs end-to-end against a US portfolio with the same code paths used for India.

Dependencies: M14.

See docs/implementation.md section M15 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- Full US portfolio run completes; report lands at reports/us/YYYY-MM-DD-weekly-report.md with §16.1 sections.
- Jhunjhunwala persona is either not in the US personas_enabled list or correctly self-skips on US tickers.
- SEC Tier-1 tools cited in at least one US stock file [Sources] line.
notes:
Risks:
- US philosophy may not yet reflect the user's actual approach (it is explicitly a template in SPEC §8.1). Mitigation: this milestone is partly a content task; no US runs produce useful output until the philosophy is real.

title: M13: Runtime Phase 7 (Report Assembly and Ledger)
description:
Goal: The weekly report exists on disk, the ledger row is committed, and the context ledger is rotated — all atomically enough that a partial run is obvious.

Dependencies: M4, M7, M12.

See docs/implementation.md section M13 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- reports/india/YYYY-MM-DD-weekly-report.md contains every section from SPEC §16.1 in order.
- ledger-ctl get-run <id> returns the row types and counts listed in SPEC §16.2.
- Modifying philosophy.md between two runs causes the second /phase1 to emit a 'philosophy changed' prompt; the stored philosophy_hash on the previous runs row differs from the computed one for the new philosophy.
- context/india/last-run.md line count <= 100; last-run-backup.md equals the prior run's context.
notes:
Risks:
- Partial ledger write on interrupt leaving an inconsistent DB. Mitigation: single transaction in append-run.
- Report-parser brittleness feeding back into ledger integrity. Mitigation: parser errors loudly and the transaction is rolled back.

title: M14: Full-portfolio integration and stress test
description:
Goal: A real India portfolio completes all 7 runtime phases across 3 parallel Cursor/Claude sessions, survives a kill-mid-batch, and surfaces any MCP coverage gaps.

Dependencies: M1-M13.

See docs/implementation.md section M14 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- Full-portfolio run completes; Immediate action list has <= 2 entries.
- Parallel stress test never produces duplicate work.
- Kill-mid-batch test resumes without manual intervention other than reclaim-*-stale.
- MCP-coverage audit either passes (all 5 tickers match Screener within tolerance) or lists every discrepancy with an assigned bd issue id.
notes:
Risks:
- Cumulative prompt drift across 7 phases producing subtly wrong reports that pass individual milestone tests. Mitigation: this milestone's full-portfolio test is the only real guardrail.
- User philosophy may not actually match the YAML front-matter shape assumed in M0. Mitigation: treat this as the validation of that migration; fix back in M0's artifact, don't patch downstream.

title: M11: Runtime Phase 5 (Persona Cross-Check)
description:
Goal: Every non-ETF stock gets a ## Persona Cross-Check block; rotation is deterministic per (run_date, market); my-philosophy is always present.

Dependencies: M3 (persona modules), M7 (persona markdown files), M10.

See docs/implementation.md section M11 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- For a fixed (run_date, market) pair, two successive Phase 5 runs select the same persona roster.
- my-philosophy appears first in every ## Persona Cross-Check block (invariant 16).
- ETF tickers have no rotating-persona lines and include the ETF-skip note.
- Disagreement between my-philosophy and any rotating persona sets a Flags: debate-trigger-reason line for M12 to consume.
notes:
Risks:
- Rotation seeded from non-stable input (wall clock, run id) breaks reproducibility. Mitigation: seed is (run_date, market) only, nothing else.
- Persona prompts drifting toward each other and producing near-identical verdicts. Mitigation: each persona file keeps its distinct threshold/weight block from SPEC §8.2 step 3.

title: M12: Runtime Phase 6 (Synthesis and Selective Debate)
description:
Goal: Concentration sanity runs, debate triggers fire exactly on SPEC §11.2 rules, and portfolio-analysis.md is assembled.

Dependencies: M3 (concentration-check), M6 (benchmark), M7 (debate templates), M11.

See docs/implementation.md section M12 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- Each of the 4 trigger fixtures yields a ## Debate section in the corresponding stock file.
- The score->=-80-unanimous fixture and the score-<=-30-dealbreaker fixture both skip debate and have no ## Debate section.
- Immediate-action list in portfolio-analysis.md has at most 2 entries, even if more triggers were present (SPEC §3.5 cap, invariant 10).
- concentration-check.md reports HHI + per-stock (current_pct, vol_adjusted_limit, delta) + top-5 correlation pairs.
notes:
Risks:
- Over-triggering debate and drowning the report in noise. Mitigation: the skip rules for high-conviction unanimous and clear dealbreakers are mandatory (SPEC §11.2), enforced by fixtures.
- Resolver introducing facts not raised in round 1/2. Mitigation: debate template's closing instructions explicitly forbid this; phase-6 prompt references invariant 'no new facts' by name.

title: M9: Runtime Phase 2 (Research) single-stock slice
description:
Goal: A single ticker (RELIANCE.NS) passes all the way through Phase 2 and produces a valid [Tag] stock file.

Dependencies: M1, M3, M7, M8.

See docs/implementation.md section M9 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- single-stock.sh produces temp/research/stocks/RELIANCE.NS.md containing [Overview], [Fund], [Valu], [Biz], [News], [Phil], [Prev], [Sources] — all with period annotations.
- NIFTYBEES.NS test produces a stock file on the lighter ETF path per SPEC §9.8.
- Stock-file linter passes on both.
- After Phase 2, portfolio-snapshot.csv is byte-identical to the Phase 1 output.
notes:
Risks:
- MCP partial-data returning empty strings instead of N/A. Mitigation: the aggregator normalizes to N/A and logs the missing fields to the stock file's missing_fields (SPEC §18.2).
- Web search leaking fabricated numbers. Mitigation: the linter rejects numbers lacking a [Sources] entry.

title: M10: Runtime Phases 3 and 4 (Verification and Scoring)
description:
Goal: Stock files pass an automated verification gate and then receive a ## Scoring block produced by parallel scoring sessions.

Dependencies: M1 (scoring claim variants), M3, M7, M9.

See docs/implementation.md section M10 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- Verification rejects a deliberately corrupted stock file missing period annotations and logs each violation in temp/research/verification-notes.md.
- On the fundamentals-first fixture, the emitted action is Hold or Hold-with-Conviction, never Exit or Trim.
- On the dealbreaker fixture, philosophy_fit is 0 and the Action reflects the break per SPEC §9.3.
- 3-session parallel scoring run completes with exactly one ## Scoring block per ticker and no duplicate writes.
notes:
Risks:
- [Fund] parsing is fragile to whitespace drift. Mitigation: the extractor is strict and errors loudly on ambiguity.
- Agent tempted to re-derive thresholds instead of quoting from the scoring-engine output (SPEC §9.3). Mitigation: phase-4 prompt includes invariant 15 (deterministic math, LLM narrative) by number.

title: M8: Runtime Phase 1 (Setup) end-to-end
description:
Goal: /phase1 market=india scope_type=market scope_value=india runs from a fresh session and produces every artifact SPEC §10.2 requires.

Dependencies: M2, M4, M5, M7.

See docs/implementation.md section M8 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- After a clean rm -rf temp/research && /phase1 market=india scope_type=market scope_value=india, these files exist: portfolio-snapshot.csv, warnings/corp-actions.md, manifest.md, and claims/{TICKER}/ for every snapshot row.
- Snapshot CSV column headers match SPEC §7.1.1 exactly.
- Re-running /phase1 without cleaning temp/research/ produces a clear error ('previous run artifacts present — clean or continue explicitly'), not a silent overwrite.
- Snapshot-freeze invariant: intentionally mutating portfolio-snapshot.csv after Phase 1 produces a tests/invariants/snapshot-frozen.sh violation that later phases must detect.
notes:
Risks:
- Accidentally calling wealthfolio-query twice and ending up with two mutually-inconsistent snapshots (e.g. price refresh between calls). Mitigation: Phase 1 writes the snapshot once and all downstream steps read from the CSV, not from Wealthfolio directly (SPEC §19.12).

title: M7: Agent runtime skeleton (prompts + commands)
description:
Goal: Every markdown file the agent reads during a run exists and follows the directory layout in SPEC §17.

Dependencies: M0.

See docs/implementation.md section M7 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- Opening .agents/portfolio-research/phases/phase-1-setup.md shows a numbered list of steps (no more than two paragraphs of prose at the top).
- Grepping all phase files returns zero occurrences of Latest, Current, or TTM as literal examples (these are forbidden per SPEC §19 inv. 7 and §10.4 check 3).
- The 21 SPEC invariants are discoverable: 5 in CLAUDE.md, the remaining 16 referenced by number in the phase prompt where they are actionable.
- Each slash command file is <= 20 lines.
notes:
Risks:
- Instruction-following cliff as prompts accumulate (SPEC drafting note, reinforced by the original Humanlayer guidance). Mitigation: each phase prompt embeds only its invariants; CLAUDE.md stays <= 80 lines.
- Prompt drift between phases that share variables (e.g. [Tag] format). Mitigation: templates are the single source; phase prompts reference the template, do not inline it.

title: M5: corp-actions-monitor
description:
Goal: Every Phase 1 run surfaces upcoming and recent corp-action events for held tickers as an informational feed, sourced from market data only. The tool never reads from or writes to Wealthfolio.

Dependencies: M0 (MCP registration).

See docs/implementation.md section M5 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- Fixture run produces a markdown table listing the seeded upcoming + past-90d events for held tickers, with source attribution and no severity column.
- With NseKit-MCP disabled, the tool falls back to Yahoo and the output lists the degradation under a 'Source degradation' note.
- Tool does not open the Wealthfolio DB at all (verified by absence of any file handle on the DB path during test runs).
- Exit code is 0 on successful scan; non-zero only on tool failure.
notes:
Risks:
- MCP coverage for Indian bonuses / mergers is uneven. When NseKit-MCP is unavailable, the tool uses Yahoo `actions` as a best-effort fallback and flags `source: Yahoo fallback` in the output; the informational feed is understood to be best-effort.

title: M6: benchmark
description:
Goal: Alpha numbers per standard window are computable programmatically for India and US portfolios.

Dependencies: M0, M2 (TWR source).

See docs/implementation.md section M6 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- Fixture portfolio produces an alpha that matches the hand-computed value within 1 basis point.
- Running twice with the same inputs produces identical output (determinism modulo Yahoo price revisions; in tests, prices are stubbed).
- Markdown output contains the phrase 'price-return (no dividend reinvestment)' adjacent to ^NSEI rows.
- When the requested window exceeds available daily_account_valuation history, output marks that window `insufficient_history` and the overall exit code remains 0.
notes:
Risks:
- Yahoo rate limits and occasional ticker outages. Mitigation: cache raw price arrays under temp/research/ for the run so retries are free.
- Portfolio TWR is derived from Wealthfolio's daily_account_valuation snapshots as the sole source; there is no transaction-derived fallback. If a requested window lacks sufficient snapshot history, that window degrades to insufficient_history rather than failing the run.

title: M4: ledger-ctl and memory-query
description:
Goal: Append-only SQLite FTS5 ledger exists, accepts a parsed weekly report, and is searchable.

Dependencies: M0.

See docs/implementation.md section M4 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- ledger-ctl init creates the exact schema in §7.5, including the report_fts virtual table with content='runs', content_rowid='id'.
- Appending a synthetic report with 10 holdings, 3 personas each, and 8 actions produces 1 runs row, 10 stock_scores, 30 persona_assessments, 8 actions, and 1 report_fts row.
- memory-query "promoter pledge" returns the synthetic run with a non-zero relevance score.
- Attempting ledger-ctl update-run (or any non-append command) is a documented error — command does not exist.
notes:
Risks:
- FTS5 content='runs' wiring — easy to misconfigure so that report_fts is disconnected from runs.id. Mitigation: dedicated test inserts a row and searches it.
- Report-template drift breaking the parser silently. Mitigation: parser rejects missing required sections with an explicit error listing the missing header.

title: M3: scoring-engine (ported from ai-hedge-fund)
description:
Goal: All deterministic scoring math — threshold checks, persona base scores, concentration math — is reproducible from fixtures with zero LLM involvement.

Dependencies: M0 (YAML front-matter parse), M2 (for later integration fixtures).

See docs/implementation.md section M3 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- scoring-engine check-thresholds --philosophy input/india/philosophy.md --scheme non_financial --metrics tests/scoring-engine/infy.json produces JSON equal to SPEC §9.3 (modulo ordering).
- Promoter check for an IT MNC uses effective_threshold: 10 and marks pass_with_exception: true.
- scoring-engine persona --persona jhunjhunwala --metrics tests/scoring-engine/infy.json is deterministic (two runs produce identical sub_scores, weighted_score, signal).
- scoring-engine concentration-check returns HHI, per-stock vol-adjusted limits, and a correlation matrix given a 10-ticker holdings.json fixture.
- Fundamentals-first invariant (SPEC §9.2) test: a 35/35 + 20/20 + 20/20 + 0/15 + 0/10 = 75 input resolves to 'Conviction Hold', never lower.
notes:
Risks:
- ai-hedge-fund upstream may refactor; pin an SHA and do not track HEAD.
- Banking/NBFC scheme differs enough from non-financial that a naive port mis-scores banks. Mitigation: dedicated HDFCBank fixture.
- Dealbreaker edge cases (zero-promoter but structurally exempt like MCX/BSE). Mitigation: sector_exceptions.stock_exchanges.exempt: true test path.

title: M1: claim-ctl
description:
Goal: Atomic per-stock claim locks work across parallel Cursor/Claude sessions on the user's machine.

Dependencies: M0 (directory contract).

See docs/implementation.md section M1 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- The 3-process concurrent test terminates with each of the 15 tickers claimed by exactly one agent and no .claimed collisions.
- complete-scoring.sh INFY.NS exits non-zero when temp/research/stocks/INFY.NS.md is missing ## Scoring.
- reclaim-stale.sh 30 releases locks whose mtime is older than 30 minutes and leaves fresh ones alone.
- shellcheck skills/scripts/*.sh passes.
notes:
Risks:
- macOS + networked filesystem: O_EXCL is not atomic over NFS. Mitigation: README forbids running the tool tree from network-mounted workspaces; acceptance test runs on local filesystem only.
- Signal-killed agents leaving zombie claim directories. Mitigation: reclaim-stale covers the recovery path.

title: M2: wealthfolio-query
description:
Goal: A read-only, named-parameter SQL wrapper is the single entry point for all Wealthfolio reads.

Dependencies: M0 (schema dump, pinned release).

See docs/implementation.md section M2 for the full deliverables list.
acceptance_criteria:
- wealthfolio-query export-snapshot --market india --scope-type market --scope-value india --theses input/india/theses.yaml --output /tmp/snap.csv produces a CSV whose header exactly matches SPEC §7.1.1.
- Attempting a write via the wrapper (e.g. injecting UPDATE) fails because of -readonly.
- Every named query in the SQL file has a matching subcommand and a passing test against the fixture.
notes:
Risks:
- The pinned Wealthfolio release may not expose a quote-refresh command; export-snapshot then relies on the user doing that in the app before invoking us (SPEC §5.4). Mitigation: document explicitly; surface as a Phase 1 preflight check in M8.
- Column naming drift across Wealthfolio minor versions. Mitigation: the versioned SQL file is the compatibility layer (SPEC §19.14).

title: Clean stale and exploratory content from SPEC.md
description: Review docs/SPEC.md, identify sections that are historical research, fallback exploration, extension/backlog material, or otherwise no longer part of the committed direction, then trim or remove them after confirmation while preserving the core product spec.
close_reason: Closed

title: Align SPEC with brainstorming decisions
description: Update docs/SPEC.md to reflect the 2026-04-17 brainstorming decisions: normalized run snapshot CSV entrypoint, run scoping, thesis sidecar source of truth, non-blocking corporate actions, ETF analysis direction, and corrected Wealthfolio version assumptions.
close_reason: Closed

title: Spec review and brainstorming note
description: Capture open questions, resolved decisions, and follow-up research items from reviewing docs/SPEC.md with the user before implementation.
close_reason: Spec review complete; work captured in plans/research

title: G8: finstack key_ratios dividend block nulled for MSFT (per-ticker data gap)
description:
Confirmed 2026-04-22 in M0.3 US smoke test. finstack-mcp 0.10.0:

key_ratios(MSFT).dividend = { dividend_rate: null, dividend_yield: null, payout_ratio: null, ex_dividend_date: null }

MSFT does pay a dividend (~$0.83/qtr at test date). Per-ticker gap — unrelated to the bank-schema issue.

Contrast:
  - key_ratios(AAPL).dividend populated (dividend_rate=1.04, dividend_yield=0.39, payout_ratio=0.1304, ex_dividend_date=1770595200).
  - key_ratios(JPM).dividend populated.

Evidence: research/mcp-smoke-test-us.md §2 C12. Canonical matrix row: research/mcp-gaps.md G8.

Severity: low. Downstream impact (M9 aggregator for MSFT): Phase 2 Valuation block dividend fields come up null for MSFT and must be sourced from Tier-B (Yahoo info.dividendRate / dividendYield). Per SPEC §18.2 the aggregator already normalizes missing fields into missing_fields[] with source attribution; this is the happy path of that mechanism.
